description: tests/integration/daemon/crash_recovery.rs and others shell out to the store unlock CLI just to remove locks. Expose a test helper that calls StoreLock::unlock directly (or reuse daemon/store lock API) to avoid extra CLI process.
title: Replace store unlock CLI calls in tests with direct helper
Set slow-tests CI timeout to 10 minutes

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] daemon test data-dir overrides isolate both daemon-owned and git-owned store/cache paths
- [ ] `cargo test --all-features -p beads-daemon load_from_checkpoint_ref --lib -- --nocapture` passes
- [ ] `cargo test` passes from the repo root on the current stack
- [ ] temporary debugging instrumentation is removed before close
description:
**Problem**
The final closeout `cargo test` run is failing in `crates/beads-daemon/src/runtime/core/mod.rs` checkpoint-ref tests, but the root cause is not checkpoint merge logic. The daemon test harness only overrides `beads-daemon::paths`, while checkpoint cache state now lives under `beads-git::paths`. Because the tests reuse a deterministic `store_id`, `load_checkpoint_imports()` can pick up a stale global cache entry before reading the repo ref, causing false positives/negatives in checkpoint import tests.

**Evidence**
On 2026-03-13, targeted reproduction with `cargo test --all-features -p beads-daemon load_from_checkpoint_ref --lib -- --nocapture` showed:
- checkpoint group snapshot and git ref exist for the test repo
- `load_checkpoint_imports()` returned a cached checkpoint containing unrelated bead `bd-cache-valid`
- expected repo-published checkpoint state was skipped because cache lookup won first

**Why It Matters**
This blocks the required `cargo test` gate for `beads-rs-gou3.8`, and it makes daemon tests non-isolated after the crate split.

**Files**
- `crates/beads-daemon/src/paths.rs`
- `crates/beads-daemon/src/runtime/core/mod.rs`
- any daemon tests/helpers that rely on `override_data_dir_for_tests()`
design:
1. Fix the daemon test data-dir override at the owner seam so it also scopes git-owned checkpoint cache paths.
2. Remove temporary debug instrumentation once the root cause is confirmed.
3. Prove the fix with the targeted checkpoint-ref tests, then rerun the full `cargo test` gate.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
Audit 2026-03-13 from /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs.

Landed in jj commit `42f4dc50` (`beads-rs-gou3.8.2: isolate checkpoint cache test overrides`). Root cause was cross-crate test isolation drift after the split: daemon tests overrode `beads-daemon::paths`, but checkpoint cache now resolves through `beads-git::paths`, so deterministic `store_id` tests could pick up stale global cache entries. Fixed the owner seam so daemon test data-dir overrides also scope git-owned checkpoint cache paths.

Targeted proof:
- `cargo test --all-features -p beads-daemon load_from_checkpoint_ref --lib -- --nocapture`

Full verification on current stack:
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `just dylint`
- `cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test`
- `cargo test --features slow-tests`
- `cargo check -p beads-rs --all-features`

title: Fix daemon test data-dir override so checkpoint cache isolation matches the new crate split
